concerns.text: FR-010 mandates Bonferroni correction with adjusted p < 0.01. The Idea's 'Expected results' section specifies 'p < 0.01, Bonferroni-adjusted' as a success metric for the final model, not necessarily as a hard filter for the differential expression (DE) step. The Idea's 'Methodology sketch' only specifies 'FDR < 0.05' for DE. Applying Bonferroni at the DE stage (thousands of genes) is extremely conservative and may yield zero genes, conflicting with the 'Expected results' goal of finding a panel. This appears to be a scope drift or over-reach in the DE step compared to the Idea's methodology.

The Assumptions section states: 'Any decision threshold... is justified... and sensitivity analysis sweeps thresholds over {0.01, 0.05, 0.1} for FDR...'. This contradicts FR-005 which hard-codes 'FDR < 0.05' and FR-010 which hard-codes 'Bonferroni... < 0.01'. The spec requires a sensitivity analysis in assumptions but hard-codes the values in requirements, creating a logical contradiction in the scope of work.
